description: Author pt-BR domain glossary + dialect ruleset (data file) mapping every domain term to its Brazilian translation (ticket→chamado, billing→faturamento, asset→ativo, etc.) and listing forbidden European-PT forms (comboio, telemóvel, ecrã, autocarro, registo, etc.) and the allowlist of strings legitimately identical to English (Status, Dashboard, Email, ...).
Add localized-loanword allowlist so the audit does not flag intentionally-identical strings (proper nouns, codes, units).

Build a pt-BR audit script (scripts/audit-pt-br.cjs) that, per namespace, reports: keys still identical to English (excluding allowlist), glossary/forbidden-term violations, and unverified (not-yet-reviewed) keys; emits a per-namespace JSON+markdown report the loop consumes.
Audit script tracks a per-key "reviewed" ledger (e.g. a sidecar pt review-state file) so the full re-audit of already-translated keys is resumable across loop iterations.
Add native-review export generator (scripts/export-pt-review.cjs) producing an en→pt side-by-side sheet (CSV/markdown) for human sign-off.

Relabel picker: set localeNames.pt = 'Português (Brasil)' in packages/core/src/lib/i18n/config.ts; pt stays in supportedLocales and INCOMPLETE_LOCALES (gated pending human review).
Keep pt in INCOMPLETE_LOCALES (do NOT auto un-gate); document the one-line removal as the post-review go-live step.
Whole-catalog gate: validate-translations.cjs and audit-pt-br.cjs both pass clean for pt across all 45 namespaces; generate the final native-review export.

Add pt blocks to the source-of-truth email template files for: email-verification, password-reset, portal-invitation, tenant-recovery, no-account-found, ticket-created, ticket-created-client, ticket-assigned, ticket-updated, ticket-updated-client, ticket-closed, ticket-comment-added (subject + html_content + text_content), translated to pt-BR per glossary, {{variables}} preserved.

Add pt blocks to the source-of-truth email template files for: invoice-generated, invoice-email, payment-received, payment-overdue, credits-expiring, project-created, project-updated, project-closed, project-assigned, project-task-assigned-primary, project-task-assigned-additional, task-updated, milestone-completed (subject + html_content + text_content), translated to pt-BR per glossary, {{variables}} preserved.

Add pt blocks to the source-of-truth email template files for: appointment-request-received, appointment-request-approved, appointment-request-declined, new-appointment-request, appointment-assigned-technician, time-entry-submitted, time-entry-approved, time-entry-rejected, survey-ticket-closed (subject + html_content + text_content), translated to pt-BR per glossary, {{variables}} preserved.

Create production migration server/migrations/<ts>_add_portuguese_email_templates.cjs that upserts all 41 source-of-truth pt rows into system_email_templates (language_code=pt, correct notification_subtype_id), mirroring the dev seed template list; idempotent up()/down().
Verify the dev seed (server/seeds/dev/68_add_notification_templates.cjs via upsertEmailTemplate) picks up the new pt blocks so fresh DBs get pt automatically.

Create migration server/migrations/<ts>_add_portuguese_internal_notification_templates.cjs inserting pt rows (language_code=pt, translated title+message, {{variables}} intact) for every internal_notification_subtype, mirroring the Polish notification migration; idempotent up()/down().

Add an automated parity check (test/script) asserting pt template coverage equals en for both system_email_templates and internal_notification_templates, and that {{variable}} sets match per template.
